title: [cm/unique-constraint] entityBundleFieldInfo

given: two records that each satisfy a server-side uniqueness invariant in isolation
when: both are created independently (offline / concurrent replicas)
then: the port's convergence rule for the uniqueness violation is observed and asserted

why_non_obvious: intent-CM graded this unique-constraint site 'hard': [unique-constraint] The `UniqueBirthLog` constraint assumes the asset birth-log value is globally unique at write time, which cannot be guaranteed under eventual consistency when concurrent replicas accept the same value without coordination or a conflict-resolution rule.

title: [source] A specified mother is appended as a child's parent only when that child has no p

given: A birth log references a mother asset and one or more child assets.
One child has an empty `parent` reference list.
Another child already has a parent, potentially not the specified mother.
A referenced child can be a non-animal asset.
when: The birth log is inserted or updated.
then: The mother is appended only to each child whose parent list was empty.
No mother is added to a child with any existing parent.
The same empty-parent rule is applied even to a non-animal referenced child.

why_non_obvious: “Sync mother” could plausibly be implemented as ensuring the birth-log mother is present. Instead, any existing parent—even a different one—blocks the addition. Also, unlike birthdate synchronization, the parent branch has no animal-bundle condition.

source_read.rule: A specified mother is appended as a child's parent only when that child has no parents at all; an existing parent list is never supplemented or reconciled, and this parent behavior is not limited to animal children.

title: [source] `soil_texture` is a normal persisted lab-test field for every lab-test type; the

given: A lab-test log has both `lab_test_type` and `soil_texture` fields.
The selected test type is not `soil`.
when: The log is created or updated, including through a non-form writer or by retaining an existing soil-texture value.
then: `soil_texture` remains a valid persisted field value; only the standard form hides its input when `lab_test_type` is not exactly `soil`.

why_non_obvious: The form comment and visibility rule imply that soil texture belongs exclusively to soil tests, but the field definition is unconditional and there is no validation or clearing logic tied to the selected type.

source_read.rule: `soil_texture` is a normal persisted lab-test field for every lab-test type; the `soil` condition only controls client-side form visibility.

title: [source] A material quantity may reference multiple material-type taxonomy terms, not jus

given: A material quantity exists.
Two distinct taxonomy terms belong to the `material_type` vocabulary/bundle.
when: Both terms are assigned to the quantity's `material_type` field.
then: The quantity retains both material-type references rather than replacing one with the other.

why_non_obvious: The singular field name and label, `material_type` / “Material type,” plausibly suggest a single-valued classification, but the field is explicitly multi-valued.

source_read.rule: A material quantity may reference multiple material-type taxonomy terms, not just one material category.

title: [source] A log matches the material-type filter if any quantity reference on the log poin

given: An input log references multiple quantities.
At least one referenced quantity revision has material type A.
Another referenced quantity does not have material type A.
when: The view is filtered for material type A.
then: The log is included exactly once.

why_non_obvious: A port could model this as a direct field on the log, require all quantities to match, or emit one result per matching quantity. The source instead uses an existential subquery specifically to avoid duplicate logs.

source_read.rule: A log matches the material-type filter if any quantity reference on the log points to a quantity revision whose material type is selected; the result is one log even if several referenced quantities match.

title: [source] For multiple selected material types, matching is OR/IN semantics: a log is incl

given: A log references a quantity of material type A.
The log has no quantity of material type B.
when: The view filter selects both A and B.
then: The log is included.

why_non_obvious: The exposed filter is multi-select, but a reimplementation could interpret multiple selections as intersecting constraints. The handler explicitly changes from equality to SQL IN when more than one value is supplied.

source_read.rule: The material-type filter is dynamically available only on the farm_log view’s page_type and page_asset displays when the view arguments contain 'input'; it is not a universal log-list filter.

title: [source] `transplant_days` is added only to newly created default edit displays for `plan

given: The entity is a taxonomy term in bundle `plant_type`.
A form display configuration exists or is being created.

why_non_obvious: A port may infer that every plant type exposes a transplant-days input, but the alteration is deliberately conditional on both the `default` form mode and the display configuration being new.

source_read.rule: `transplant_days` is added only to newly created default edit displays for `plant_type` taxonomy terms; existing/custom display configurations are not modified.

title: [source] Medical logs add a bundle-specific optional `vet` field whose value is an uncons

given: A log has the `medical` type.
A veterinarian may or may not have been involved.
when: A veterinarian name is recorded on the medical log.
then: The medical-specific value is stored as the free-text `vet` string.
No veterinarian entity identity or relationship is required.
The value may be absent when no veterinarian was involved.

why_non_obvious: A field named “Veterinarian” could plausibly be implemented as a structured practitioner, contact, or user reference. The source explicitly defines it as a string and frames it as conditional free-text entry only when a veterinarian was involved.

source_read.rule: Medical logs add a bundle-specific optional `vet` field whose value is an unconstrained string representing the veterinarian's name, rather than a reference to a veterinarian/user/entity.

title: [source] Material-type matching is revision-sensitive: it checks the material type on the

given: A log references quantity Q at revision 1, whose material type is A.
Quantity Q is later revised so its current material type is B.
when: The view is filtered for A.
then: The log matches based on its recorded reference to revision 1.

why_non_obvious: A local read model may naturally join a quantity ID to its current projection. The source joins both the referenced quantity ID and referenced quantity revision ID to the material-type revision row.

source_read.rule: Material-type matching is revision-sensitive: it checks the material type on the exact quantity revision recorded by each log’s quantity reference, rather than necessarily the quantity’s latest material type.

title: [source] Laboratory references are limited to taxonomy terms in the `lab` vocabulary/bund

given: A lab-test log is assigned a laboratory.
The referenced laboratory term does not already exist.
when: The laboratory field is saved through a path honoring the field's auto-create behavior.
then: The value is a reference to a taxonomy term constrained to the `lab` bundle, and a new matching term may be created rather than rejecting the value.

why_non_obvious: An entity-reference field named Laboratory could be ported as a reference to a pre-existing laboratory record only, or as a free-text value; this definition imposes a target subtype while also allowing implicit creation.

source_read.rule: Laboratory references are limited to taxonomy terms in the `lab` vocabulary/bundle, but a missing matching term may be created automatically.

title: [source] `transplant_days` is rendered only in newly created full view displays for `plan

given: The entity is a taxonomy term in bundle `plant_type`.
A `full` view display configuration is being newly created.

why_non_obvious: The field name could lead an implementer to expose it universally, and the form configuration uses a generic number widget, while the view configuration explicitly uses integer formatting and is constrained to new `full` displays.

source_read.rule: `transplant_days` is rendered only in newly created full view displays for `plant_type` taxonomy terms, as an inline-label integer with prefix/suffix enabled; it is not automatically added to other view modes or existing display configurations.

title: [source] A transplanting log is identified solely as the registered log-type ID `transpla

given: A consumer can create or project farm logs by registered log type.
A log has type ID `transplanting`.
when: The log is handled through this module's transplanting log-type plugin.

why_non_obvious: The module name and log type suggest transplanting-specific operational semantics (for example dates, plant types, or transplant-day calculations), but the actual plugin is only a typed registration extending the generic farm log behavior.

source_read.rule: A transplanting log is identified solely as the registered log-type ID `transplanting`; this module defines no additional transplant-specific fields, state transitions, calculations, or validation behavior in the log-type class.

title: [source] Lab test type values are dynamic configuration IDs mapped to their current label

given: Lab test type configuration entities exist with IDs and labels.
A lab test form or field requests allowed values.
then: Every loaded configuration entity contributes an option whose stored ID is the value and whose current label is the display text.
The options are not explicitly sorted by ID or label.

why_non_obvious: A field named "Test type" can look like a stable built-in enumeration (for example, soil), but its permitted values come from all stored lab-test-type config entities and retain storage iteration order.

source_read.rule: Lab test type values are dynamic configuration IDs mapped to their current labels, not a fixed enum; no explicit sorting is applied when producing options.

title: [source] A seeding log carries lot number and source as strings, while purchase date is a

given: A seeding log has a lot number, purchase date, and source recorded.
when: The log is persisted and later materialized.
then: lot_number and source are represented as string values.
purchase_date is represented as a timestamp value, not merely display-formatted date text.

why_non_obvious: Field labels suggest ordinary text/date inputs, but the storage value types differ; a local event schema that stores all three as strings can lose timestamp semantics for purchase date.

source_read.rule: A seeding log carries lot number and source as strings, while purchase date is a timestamp value rather than a date-only string.

title: [source] Saving a birth log synchronizes each referenced animal child's birthdate to the

given: A log has bundle `birth` and references one or more assets in its `asset` field.
One referenced asset is an `animal` whose `birthdate` differs from the log `timestamp`.
Another referenced asset may be a non-animal.
when: The birth log is inserted or updated.
then: The animal's `birthdate` becomes exactly the birth log timestamp.
The non-animal is not changed by the birthdate synchronization.
A child is saved only if at least one synchronization changed it.

why_non_obvious: A reimplementation may treat birthdate as immutable at birth-log creation, or assume every referenced child asset receives the field update. The hook runs for inserts and updates, but explicitly gates this particular synchronization on the child's animal bundle.

source_read.rule: The animal birthdate display links to an access-visible birth log that references the animal, but if multiple such logs exist it uses the query's first returned ID without an explicit ordering or latest-log selection.

title: [source] Material-type references may create a new material-type term when the referenced

given: A material quantity is being recorded.
The supplied material-type value has no existing matching taxonomy term.
when: The value is assigned through the material-type reference input.
then: A new `material_type` taxonomy term may be created and referenced by the quantity.

why_non_obvious: An entity-reference field normally implies selection from existing entities; `auto_create` changes write semantics by permitting creation as part of assignment.

source_read.rule: Material-type references may create a new material-type term when the referenced value does not already exist.

title: [source] A material quantity inherits all default fields and semantics of the base farm q

given: A quantity is of type `material`.
when: Its schema is built or persisted.
then: It includes the base farm-quantity fields plus the `material_type` reference field.

why_non_obvious: A port based only on the specialized type name may implement only material classification and omit the generic quantity fields, because this class declares only its extension field locally.

source_read.rule: A material quantity inherits all default fields and semantics of the base farm quantity type; material type is additive rather than a replacement quantity schema.

title: [domain-logic] FarmLogType

given: the domain state this class operates on
then: the value it computes / maintains is observed at the boundary

why_non_obvious: A high-reach domain class concentrates behavior many members depend on; its non-obvious rules are load-bearing for a faithful port.

title: [source] A harvest log has an optional lot_number value stored as a string; it is metadat

given: A log is of type harvest.
The harvest may or may not belong to a batch or lot.
when: A lot identifier is recorded for the harvest.
then: The value is retained as the harvest's lot_number string.
No numeric semantics, entity-reference semantics, or mandatory-value behavior is implied.

why_non_obvious: “Lot number” can plausibly be modeled as a numeric field, a reference to a lot record, or a required batch identifier. The source defines only a plain string field and specifies no required constraint.

source_read.rule: A harvest log has an optional lot_number value stored as a string; it is metadata on the harvest log, not a separate lot/batch entity or a numeric identifier.

title: [hook-implementation] ThemeHooks

given: the entity state the hook keys on
when: the lifecycle event that triggers the hook occurs
then: the hook's derived side effect is observable at the value boundary

why_non_obvious: A hook fires a side effect on an entity lifecycle event. A re-implementer who models only the primary write can miss the derived state the hook maintains.

title: [views-filter] QuantityViewsData

given: records that the filter would include and exclude
then: only the included records are returned, in the filter's order

why_non_obvious: A Views query/filter shapes what a read returns (visibility, ordering, exclusion). The filter semantics are invisible from the read's method name.
